name: agent-ready adoption recipes
description: Copy-paste workflows for agents that need to orient, inspect risk, validate, and hand off work in an agent-ready repository. Maintainer setup is covered by docs/adoption-playbook.md.
safetyRules: Run context before editing so repo facts, docs, commands, and risks are loaded first.
Run status when you need one dashboard for readiness, current changes, validation, recipes, and CI adoption.
Run workspaces in monorepos before choosing package-local or repo-wide validation.
Run affected when you only need workspace packages touched by current or planned paths.
Run impact before editing known risky or workspace-owned paths, or before handoff with changed=true.
Use affectedPackages from impact and preflight to choose package-scoped validation in monorepos.
Use agent-ready run only for discovered or configured command names.
Pass --allow-network only when the command metadata requires network and the user has approved it.
Pass --allow-writes only when the command metadata writes files and the user has approved it.
Keep MCP usage read-only; command execution happens through the CLI.

steps.expect: Read readiness, current git changes, validation receipts, recipe access, and CI adoption hints.
Read status, purpose, docs, commands, risk paths, MCP surface, and recommended workflow.
In monorepos, identify affected packages and package-scoped commands before choosing validation.
Identify workspace packages touched by planned paths without running the full impact report.
Stop for blocking readiness failures; report warnings before editing if strict mode fails.
Review path risk, affected workspace packages, validation recommendations, and handoff notes before editing.
Use current git changes to combine doctor status, impact, affected workspace packages, validation, and handoff guidance.
Attach the structured command receipt to the final handoff or use it to decide the next validation step.
Generate transfer notes with changed paths, risks, validation guidance, and remaining work.
Load the standard orientation prompt for the task focus.
Read the compact onboarding packet.
In monorepos, identify workspace packages and package-scoped command suggestions.
Identify workspace packages touched by planned paths without running the full impact report.
Confirm generated docs and metadata are ready.
Check planned path risk and affected workspace packages before editing.
Check readiness and changed-path guidance before transfer.
Use the CLI for command execution because the MCP server is read-only.
Prepare the final transfer packet.
If status is needs_validation, run the recommended command by name.
Capture ok, status, durationMs, exitCode, stdout, stderr, and sideEffects.
Summarize completed work, changed files, validation receipts, open risks, and next steps.
Review the full maintainer adoption path from local setup to CI receipts.
Review the generated GitHub Actions workflow without writing files, including status and contract receipt artifact steps.
Confirm the action reference, mode, strictness, target path, receipt artifacts, and YAML content.
Create .github/workflows/agent-ready.yml only after the user approves the workflow.
Confirm generated agent docs and metadata are current before relying on CI.
Summarize downloaded CI receipt artifacts for readiness, contract validity, and next steps.
